concepts.title: Zoom Phone Mobile
concepts.summary: A service that extends Zoom Phone to employees' mobile devices using eSIM technology, making the smartphone a native Zoom Phone endpoint. Users make and receive business calls through their phone's native dialer while retaining enterprise features (presence, voicemail, call recording) from the Zoom Phone platform. Calls can route over cellular voice networks for carrier-grade reliability.
key_points: eSIM-based integration with mobile network providers
Native dialer for calls; Zoom Workplace app for advanced features
Business and personal communication separation on one device
Centralized admin control via eSIM Management in the web portal

concepts.title: Zoom Phone Mobile License
concepts.summary: A per-user license that enables Zoom Phone Mobile service. Licenses come in Primary and Secondary line types, each with distinct data allowances and hotspot policies. The license type must match the SIM configuration on the user's device or the service is automatically deactivated.
key_points: Per-user license, assigned via eSIM Management (not User Management)
Two types: Primary line (50GB data, 20GB hotspot) and Secondary line (2GB data, no hotspot)
License type must match device SIM configuration or data connectivity is auto-deactivated
Must assign Zoom Phone Basic license in User Management before assigning Mobile license in eSIM Management

concepts.title: Primary Line
concepts.summary: The higher-tier Zoom Phone Mobile license type providing 50GB of high-speed data per billing cycle with up to 20GB of mobile hotspot usage. After data limits are reached, speeds are throttled: general data to 500 kbps, hotspot to 128 kbps. Resets each billing cycle.
key_points: 50GB high-speed data monthly
Up to 20GB high-speed mobile hotspot included
Post-limit throttling: 500 kbps general, 128 kbps hotspot
Must be configured as primary SIM on device

concepts.title: Secondary Line
concepts.summary: The lower-tier Zoom Phone Mobile license type providing 2GB of high-speed data per billing cycle with no mobile hotspot capability. After 2GB, general data speeds are reduced to 500 kbps. Suitable for lighter usage scenarios.
key_points: 2GB high-speed data monthly
No mobile hotspot available
Post-limit: 500 kbps for remainder of billing cycle
Must be configured as secondary SIM on device

concepts.title: eSIM Management
concepts.summary: The admin interface within Zoom web portal for managing Zoom Phone Mobile users, their eSIMs, numbers, and roaming. This is a separate management surface from User Management, reflecting the distinct lifecycle of mobile eSIM provisioning.
key_points: Accessible under Phone System Management > eSIM Management
Manages eSIM creation, issuance, deactivation, and reissuance
Separate from User Management—license assignment requires two-step process
Automatically creates a mobile device endpoint for the user in call handling settings

user_roles.title: Account Owner
user_roles.summary: Has full privileges to purchase Zoom Phone Mobile licenses, obtain mobile numbers, assign licenses to users, and manage eSIMs. Can control international roaming access and deactivate/activate service per user.
key_points: Can purchase and assign Zoom Phone Mobile licenses
Manages eSIM issuance and international roaming
Receives roaming control toggles per user
Automatically issued eSIM on first license assignment

user_roles.title: Admin
user_roles.summary: Admins can perform the same Zoom Phone Mobile management actions as account owners: assign licenses, issue/reissue eSIMs, manage roaming, and deactivate/activate service. Requires appropriate admin privileges for eSIM Management access.
key_points: Access to eSIM Management for license assignment and eSIM operations
Can control roaming access and deactivate/activate user service
Same capabilities as account owner within eSIM Management scope

user_roles.title: End User
user_roles.summary: Zoom Phone users who receive a Zoom Phone Mobile license. After an admin issues their eSIM, the user receives an activation email with a QR code to install on their device. Users make and receive business calls via their phone's native dialer, with advanced Zoom Phone features available through the Zoom Workplace mobile app.
key_points: Receives activation email with QR code after admin assigns license
Uses native dialer for business calls on mobile device
Accesses advanced features (presence, voicemail, recording) via Zoom Workplace app
SMS/MMS through device's native messaging app; Zoom app is read-only for messages

task_flows.title: Purchase Zoom Phone Mobile Licenses
task_flows.summary: Admins acquire Zoom Phone Mobile licenses (Primary or Secondary line types) for their organization. The purchasing flow distinguishes between UK-specific license variants (Primary with premium data, Secondary with essential data).
key_points: License type selection: Primary line or Secondary line
Regional variants exist (e.g., UK-specific SKUs)
Per-user licensing model

task_flows.title: Obtain Mobile eSIM Numbers
task_flows.summary: A two-step process where admins first create or verify a business address, then obtain mobile eSIM numbers (new or port-in). Verified business address is a gate: eSIM numbers cannot be obtained without one.
key_points: Step 1: Create or verify business address
Step 2: Obtain mobile eSIM numbers (new acquisition or port-in)
Business address verification is a prerequisite gate

task_flows.title: Assign Zoom Phone Mobile License to a User
task_flows.summary: A two-stage assignment flow: first, the admin assigns at least a Zoom Phone Basic license to the user in User Management; then, they assign the Zoom Phone Mobile license in eSIM Management. This split reflects the different lifecycle domains (user identity vs. mobile connectivity).
key_points: Stage 1: Assign Zoom Phone Basic license in User Management
Stage 2: Assign Zoom Phone Mobile license in eSIM Management
First-time assignment auto-generates an eSIM and sends QR code email to user
Mobile device endpoint auto-created in user's call handling settings

task_flows.title: Manage eSIM Activation and Roaming
task_flows.summary: Admins manage the eSIM lifecycle from eSIM Management, including issuing first-time eSIMs (automated on license assignment), reissuing eSIMs for lost devices, and deactivating/activating service. International roaming is controlled per user and is disabled by default to prevent unexpected charges.
key_points: Auto-issuance on first license assignment
Reissue for lost/stolen devices
Deactivate/activate service per user
Roaming disabled by default; admin must explicitly enable
Day-pass roaming model: only active when user actually roams

constraints.title: Device Compatibility and Carrier Lock
constraints.summary: Users must have a compatible eSIM-enabled smartphone on iOS or Android. The device must not be carrier-locked; locked devices will prevent eSIM installation and activation.
key_points: eSIM-capable smartphone required (iOS and Android supported)
Carrier-locked devices are incompatible
Design implication: pre-activation compatibility check should flag locked devices

constraints.title: SMS/MMS App Restriction
constraints.summary: SMS and MMS are only supported through the mobile device's native messaging app. The Zoom Workplace app shows messages in read-only mode but cannot send them due to iOS and Android platform restrictions.
key_points: Native messaging app for sending SMS/MMS
Zoom Workplace app: read-only message view
Important UX implication: users need clear guidance on which app to use for which function

constraints.title: Two-Stage License Assignment Requirement
constraints.summary: Admins must assign licenses in a specific order: first Zoom Phone Basic in User Management, then Zoom Phone Mobile in eSIM Management. eSIM Management cannot assign a Mobile license to a user who lacks the Basic license. This split assignment flow adds operational complexity.
key_points: Design implication: cross-surface workflow with dependency
eSIM Management should indicate which users are eligible (already have Basic license)
Error prevention: block or warn if Mobile license assigned without Basic license

constraints.title: License-SIM Configuration Mismatch Detection
constraints.summary: The Zoom Phone Mobile license type (Primary vs. Secondary) must match the SIM configuration on the device. If a mismatch is detected, the user's data connectivity is automatically deactivated, enforcing the Acceptable Fair Use Policy.
key_points: Auto-detection of mismatch between license type and device SIM config
Consequence: automatic data deactivation
Design implication: clear in-product warnings during activation, not just policy text

constraints.title: Data Speed Throttling Thresholds
constraints.summary: Both Primary and Secondary lines have hard data caps after which speeds are significantly throttled. Primary: 50GB general / 20GB hotspot. Secondary: 2GB general / no hotspot. Throttled speeds (500 kbps general, 128 kbps hotspot) persist until the billing cycle resets.
key_points: Primary: 50GB cap, throttles to 500 kbps (data) / 128 kbps (hotspot)
Secondary: 2GB cap, throttles to 500 kbps, no hotspot available
Resets at billing cycle boundary
Design implication: usage meter and threshold warnings in admin/end-user views

constraints.title: Roaming Defaults and Day-Pass Model
constraints.summary: International roaming is deactivated by default for all users to prevent bill shock. Roaming operates on a day-pass model with two tiers: included roaming locations at no extra cost, and day-pass locations that incur per-day charges.
key_points: Deactivated by default—admin must opt in per user
Day-pass only activates when user actually roams, not at purchase time
Two tiers: included roaming (free) and day-pass roaming (paid per day)

ux_patterns.title: Split Admin Surface for User vs. Mobile Management
ux_patterns.summary: User identity management and mobile license assignment occur in two separate admin interfaces (User Management vs. eSIM Management). This split-surface pattern requires admins to understand the dependency order and navigate between contexts to complete a single user's setup.
key_points: User Management handles Basic license and user identity
eSIM Management handles Mobile license, eSIM issuance, and roaming
Sequential dependency: Basic license first, then Mobile license
Design consideration: cross-link between surfaces to reduce friction

ux_patterns.title: Automatic eSIM Creation with Email Notification
ux_patterns.summary: When a Zoom Phone Mobile license is first assigned to a user, the system automatically creates an eSIM, generates a QR code, and sends a welcome email to the user. This zero-touch provisioning pattern removes manual admin steps for initial setup.
key_points: eSIM auto-created on license assignment
QR code delivered via email for user self-activation
Reduces admin burden for initial provisioning

ux_patterns.title: Auto-Created Device Endpoint in Call Handling
ux_patterns.summary: Assigning a Zoom Phone Mobile license automatically creates a mobile device endpoint in the user's call handling settings. This ensures the mobile device appears as a destination in call routing and handling configurations without additional admin setup.
key_points: Mobile endpoint auto-created on license assignment
Integrates into existing call handling and routing UX
Reduces manual device registration steps

ux_patterns.title: Roaming Opt-In with Default-Off Safety
ux_patterns.summary: International roaming is deactivated by default, requiring explicit admin opt-in per user. This default-off pattern protects against unexpected charges (bill shock) and shifts the decision burden to admins who must deliberately enable roaming.
key_points: Default-off for financial safety
Per-user toggle with optional date range limits
Design consideration: surface roaming status clearly in admin overview
